Below the hero, the layout transitions into a main-content-with-sidebar pattern: on larger screens, the main column on the left (about 65–70% width) stacks the mission/vision text, core values, outreach programs, campus list, and a closing support CTA, while a persistent right sidebar area (about 30–35% width) is reserved for global widgets such as search and the floating WhatsApp button. All of this sits above a full-width footer navigation at the bottom.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Vertically, the header occupies roughly 20–25% of the initial viewport height, the mission/vision hero image about 60–70%, and the remaining space introduces the start of the main content and sidebar. Each major section below the hero (core values, outreach programs, campus list, CTA, footer) is separated by 48–64px of vertical spacing, with internal groupings using 16–24px gaps. Horizontally, a max content width of ~1140–1200px is centered within full-width sections. Inside that, the grid approximates a 12-column system: the main content column spans about 8 columns (65–70% width) and the implied sidebar spans about 4 columns (30–35% width) on desktop, collapsing to a single column on mobile.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density is moderate and consistent with the mockup: the hero is visually rich but text-light, with a single bold headline and minimal supporting copy. Below, sections use clear headings, short paragraphs, and list structures rather than long continuous text. Cards and rows maintain comfortable padding (8–16px vertical, 16–24px horizontal) and breathing room between elements, using 16–24px internal spacing to avoid crowding while still presenting multiple items per viewport to keep it informationally efficient.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow starts at the top-left with the logo in the dark header, then moves horizontally along the high-contrast navigation on the right, following an F-pattern. From there, attention drops diagonally into the mission/vision hero headline placed left-of-center in the lower third of the image, with an accent underline guiding the eye along the text baseline. Scrolling down, the eye continues in a Z-pattern: from left-aligned section headings down through the main content column, while the right sidebar and bottom-right WhatsApp button form a secondary interaction focus. Near the bottom, a high-contrast support CTA band recaptures attention before the eye settles on the footer navigation links.

layout.overall_description: The About page presents as a modern, editorial-style nonprofit site: a full-width, nearly black header anchors the very top, with the outreach initiative’s name on the left and generously spaced navigation on the right. Directly beneath, a wide hero-style mission/vision section spans the full width, dominated by a photograph with a bold, white headline overlaid slightly left-of-center in the lower third of the image, underlined by a subtle accent bar. As the user scrolls, the layout resolves into a clean two-column structure within a centered content container: a wider left column carries the mission/vision text, core values, outreach programs, and campus information in stacked sections, while a narrower right column is reserved for search and support widgets, echoing the mockup’s sidebar feel. Cards and rows in the outreach and campus sections are aligned in a tidy grid within this main column, with ample white space between groups to keep the experience calm and readable. Near the bottom, a strong, full-width support CTA band cuts across the flow, inviting users to serve or give, before the page concludes with a dark, minimal footer bar of navigation links that visually echoes the header and neatly closes the composition.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The primary navigation bar sits fixed at the very top of the page, spanning the full browser width with a dark, high-contrast background. Within it, a centered content container of approximately 1140–1200px width aligns to the underlying 12-column grid. The left 30% of this container houses the church outreach logo and brand text, aligned flush left with 24px horizontal padding from the container edge. The right 70% holds the navigation links, right-aligned and evenly spaced with 24px–32px between each item to mirror the airy header in the mockup. Vertically, the bar is moderately tall, occupying about 80–96px, with nav links vertically centered in the bar. There is roughly 16–20px of padding above and below the text elements. The bar visually anchors the page, creating a strong horizontal band at the top that contrasts against the hero image below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Sticky at the top of the viewport on scroll, full-width, remaining fixed while content beneath scrolls.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The about-page data loader appears as a semi-transparent overlay that covers the entire viewport below the navigation bar whenever the About content is loading. It stretches from the bottom edge of the navigation bar down to the bottom of the browser window, matching the full width of the page. The overlay slightly darkens the underlying content while preserving a hint of the layout beneath, maintaining the site’s modern feel. At the center of the viewport, a compact loading indicator (spinner or subtle animation) is placed, with a short status label directly beneath it, both horizontally and vertically centered. The overlay respects the page’s max content width only for the text label; the scrim itself is full-width to clearly signal the blocked state.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed-position full-viewport overlay (beneath the nav bar), appearing above all main content layers and preventing interaction while active.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The mission-vision section begins immediately below the navigation bar and functions as the hero of the About page. It occupies the full width of the browser, with a large background photograph extending edge-to-edge horizontally and taking up roughly 60–70% of the initial viewport height. Overlaying the image, a content block is positioned slightly left-of-center horizontally and in the lower third vertically. This block spans about 40–50% of the page width, aligning to the main content column if the grid were visible. The mission headline appears in large, white text with a subtle red/magenta accent underline running beneath the main line of copy; a brief supporting sentence or tagline sits just below with 8–16px vertical spacing. Below the image, but still part of the same section, the layout transitions to a white background and a centered max-width container. Within this container, the mission and vision bodies are arranged in a two-column layout on desktop: the left half for Mission, the right half for Vision, each column spanning roughly 50% of the main content width with 24–32px internal padding and 24px vertical spacing between heading and paragraph. On smaller screens, this collapses into a single column with mission stacked above vision, each separated by 24px. The entire section is cushioned by 48–64px of vertical margin before the core values section begins.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the normal document flow; the image portion behaves as a responsive hero, cropping as needed while maintaining its height proportion, and the text overlay remains anchored relative to the image area.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The core values list sits directly beneath the mission-vision section within the same overall main content column on the left side of the layout. It starts with a left-aligned section heading positioned at the same horizontal alignment as the mission/vision text above, located about 48–64px below the bottom of the mission/vision content block. The section occupies the full width of the main content column (about 65–70% of the content container on desktop), leaving the implied right sidebar area visually free or populated by global widgets. Below the heading, a short introductory sentence spans roughly 70% of the main column width, followed by the core values displayed either in a 2- or 3-column responsive grid, depending on screen size. Each value occupies a card-like cell with a bold label at the top and a concise description below, separated by 8–12px. Each cell has 16–20px of internal padding and 16–24px of vertical spacing between rows of values, with equal horizontal gutter spacing (16–24px) between columns. The overall block height is moderate, likely occupying around half a viewport’s height on desktop, with 48–56px of vertical spacing before the outreach programs section starts.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the main content column, aligning to the left edge of the main content and wrapping into fewer columns on smaller viewports.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The outreach programs section occupies the next major band below the core values, separated by roughly 56–64px of vertical space. It spans the full allowed content width, but its internal structure emphasizes the main-content-plus-sidebar pattern. Within the centered container, the left 65–70% forms the main program area: this area starts with a left-aligned section heading and a brief description, stacked with about 8–16px between them. Below, outreach-program-card components are laid out in a responsive grid: on larger screens, typically in two cards per row (each card taking about 48% of the main column width with a 4% gutter), and potentially three per row if space allows; on smaller screens, cards stack in a single column. The right 30–35% of the container is visually reserved for the sidebar, aligning vertically with this section but containing site-wide widgets (e.g., search) that are not part of this component list. Vertical padding within the section keeps content from touching adjacent bands, with about 32–40px padding at the top and bottom inside the section background, which can subtly differ from pure white (e.g., a light neutral tint) to distinguish it from the sections above and below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, but respects a two-column grid relationship with the persistent sidebar on desktop and collapses to a single column (main content followed by sidebar) on smaller screens.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each outreach program card is a rectangular tile placed within the outreach programs grid. On desktop, a single card typically occupies about half of the main content column’s width (or one-third, depending on breakpoint), with consistent horizontal gutters of 16–24px between cards and 24px between rows. The card has a light background (white or very light gray) with a subtle border or shadow, and internal padding of 16–24px on all sides. At the top of the card, an optional thumbnail image or icon spans the full card width with an aspect ratio around 16:9, with 12–16px of space below it. The program title appears next, left-aligned, followed by 8–12px of vertical spacing and a concise description of two to three lines. At the bottom of the card, aligned either left or right, a small call-to-action such as a text link or compact button sits with about 16px of padding above it. Cards are all equal height per row where possible to maintain a clean grid; on mobile, they stretch to 100% of the available width and stack vertically with 16–24px between each.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the outreach programs grid, reflowing responsively from multi-column layout on desktop to single-column on smaller screens.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The campus list section follows the outreach programs, separated above by approximately 56–64px of vertical whitespace to keep sections distinct. It remains anchored in the left main content column, aligned to the same left edge as previous headings. The section begins with a left-aligned heading and possibly a brief explanatory line, occupying about 60–70% of the main column width. Below, campus-item-row components stack vertically in a single column within the main column, creating a clean, scannable list. The overall section width matches the main content column (roughly 65–70% of the full content container), leaving the right 30–35% for the sidebar column. Vertical padding of 32–40px at the top and bottom of this section ensures breathing room around the rows, and a subtle dividing line or alternating background may be used to visually differentiate this band from the programs section above and the CTA below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static in the main column, forming a vertical stack of rows that maintain alignment with the grid and adjust naturally to viewport width.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each campus item row sits within the campus list section as a horizontally oriented band that spans the full width of the main content column. Rows are arranged one per line, with 8–16px of vertical spacing between them. Each row has 16–20px of vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding, with a very light border or underline to separate it from adjacent rows. Inside the row, the layout is split into two main regions: on larger screens, the left two-thirds of the row display campus name, city, and brief address details stacked vertically, while the right one-third aligns one or two small action links (e.g., “View Map”, “Service Times”) either right-aligned or grouped with subtle icons. This creates a left-heavy information zone with a right-aligned interaction zone, echoing the header’s left/right balance. On smaller screens, the actions flow beneath the campus details and align left, with 8–12px of vertical spacing, keeping the row compact but readable.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the campus list, expanding horizontally to fill the main content column and reflowing its internal arrangement from two-column to stacked layout on smaller viewports.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The support outreach CTA forms a distinct, high-impact band near the bottom of the page, positioned below the campus list section and above the footer navigation, with about 64px of vertical space separating it from the content above. It spans the full width of the content container and can optionally bleed to full browser width for greater impact, visually echoing the dark header band. The background color is a strong, high-contrast tone (e.g., the same deep black or a rich accent color) that clearly stands out from the white or light sections above. Within the centered container, the CTA is horizontally oriented: on desktop, a bold, left-aligned heading and one-line supporting copy occupy the left 60% of the band; to the right, one or two prominent buttons or links (e.g., “Support Outreach”, “Give Now”, “Serve With Us”) are grouped together, aligned to the right edge of the container. Vertical padding is generous (24–32px top and bottom), giving the band a substantial, banner-like feel. On smaller screens, the heading, supporting text, and buttons stack vertically, all left-aligned, with 12–16px between each element.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static but visually anchored as a full-width or near-full-width band that stands out from the surrounding content; it does not float or stick but is designed to be a strong focal point as users reach the bottom of the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The about footer navigation sits at the very bottom of the page as a low-profile, full-width horizontal bar that visually mirrors the header’s dark color palette but with slightly reduced contrast to signal its subordinate role. It extends edge-to-edge across the browser, with a centered content container of about 1140–1200px. Inside this container, footer links are arranged in a single horizontal line on desktop, left- or center-aligned, with 16–24px spacing between each link to maintain the spacious feel. Any secondary text (such as copyright or organization info) sits either on the opposite side or below the primary link row in a lighter, smaller type. The vertical height is modest, with around 16–24px of padding above and below the link row. On smaller screens, links wrap into multiple lines or stack, remaining centered or left-aligned but always maintaining comfortable vertical spacing so the area feels tidy rather than cramped.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static at the end of the document flow as the final element of the page, spanning full width without any sticky or floating behavior.
